text:
Eva loved collecting seashells. Not just any kind. Those little translucent star shells with the most splendid patterns. One day she saw a special one at an antique store. It sparkled like an emERALDO. Eva bought it. That night she dreamed she lived there.

She floated around, admiring the ancient statues. They spoke to her. "Oh hello," one said. "You must be the collector."

"Really," Eva said. "That's kind."

"You've got something special."

A woodpecker tilted his head.

"Yes," Eva whispered.

A soft glow came from the shell.

"Come live there," the woodpecker urged.

"What do you do?" Eva asked dreamily.

Live quietly. Eat bugs.

"Eating bugs isn't that pleasant." She chuckled.

"Just listen." It felt like speaking with feathers.

"Oh." Eva felt their words.

"A cozy place."

"It feels... warm."

A flicker.

"Take care." It chirped.
